keyword_density.interpretation: Density improved from 0.27% to 0.39% (+44%). Still rates 'low' per API but combined with semantic variations ('agent orchestration' 24×, 'multi-agent systems' 28×, 'orchestrator'), the topic signal is strong. Acceptable for a 3,000-word long-form article where keyword stuffing would degrade readability.

readability.interpretation: Flesch improved from 22.9 to 27.8 (+4.9 points). Grade level dropped from 13.9 to 12.6. Average sentence length shortened from 15.4 to 13.0 words. Three plain-language interludes added (Executive Summary, Single-Agent section, Conclusion). Still technically 'Very Difficult' but the trajectory is right and the plain-language summaries provide accessibility anchors. For highly technical enterprise B2B content on AI orchestration frameworks, 27.8 is near the realistic floor — topic vocabulary (orchestration, observability, idempotency, governance) inherently drags syllable counts.

Meta description is 183 chars — slightly over Google's typical 155-160 char snippet limit, but the first 155 chars contain the primary keyword, secondary signals (LangGraph, CrewAI, n8n), and a compelling value proposition. Truncation occurs harmlessly at 'multi-age...' (end of 'multi-agent production deployments'). This is a strong SERP preview.
